title: matthiola fruticulosa var. bolleana
intro: Matthiola fruticulosa var. bolleana, also known as the Bolleana Stock or annual stock, is an annual flowering plant in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its fragrant, white, pink, or purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Matthiola fruticulosa var. bolleana include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its antiseptic properties, making it effective in wound healing and skin infections. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other medicinal products.
